output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's Your Recovery Plan
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what deductions are legal, and how a demand letter gets your money back fast.
content_outline: Introduction: Addressing the Frustration When a Landlord Is Not Returning Your Deposit
Can a Landlord Keep Your Security Deposit? Know the Legal Deductions
State-by-State Deadlines: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit
What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Give You Your Deposit Back
How to Write an Effective Security Deposit Dispute Letter
Conclusion: Take Immediate Action to Recover Your Deposit
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 45 days after move-out.
Landlords may only deduct unpaid rent and damage beyond normal wear and tear; cleaning fees must be reasonable.
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most cost-effective way to pressure a landlord into returning wrongfully withheld funds.
DepositBack automates state-specific demand letters to ensure proper legal language and maximize recovery chances.
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes with DepositBack and demand your money back today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters dealing with landlords withholding, delaying, or refusing to return security deposits and seeking urgent, actionable help.
